content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/CreateFromInstanceTypes/components/CreateVMFooter/components/ActionButtons/ActionButtons.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 66, 82, 90, 99
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 66):
   65              onClick={onCreate}
   66              variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
   67            >

  (line 82):
   81              onClick={onCustomize}
   82              variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
   83            >

  (line 90):
   89        <SplitItem>
   90          <Button onClick={onCancel} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>
   91            {t('Cancel')}

  (line 99):
   98            onClick={onViewYAML}
   99            variant={ButtonVariant.secondary}
  100          >

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 62, 78, 90, 96
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: SplitItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 62):
   61          >
   62            <Button
   63              isAriaDisabled={isCreationDisabled}

  (line 78):
   77          >
   78            <Button
   79              isDisabled={isCreationDisabled}

  (line 90):
   89        <SplitItem>
   90          <Button onClick={onCancel} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>
   91            {t('Cancel')}

  (line 96):
   95        <SplitItem>
   96          <Button
   97            isDisabled={isViewYAMLDisabled}

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 56, 72, 89, 94, 95
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-splititem-component]:
Component <SplitItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <SplitItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <SplitItem>{content} other children</SplitItem>
  After:  <SplitItem component=>other children</SplitItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: SplitItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Split

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: SplitItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 56):
   55      <Split hasGutter>
   56        <SplitItem>
   57          <HidableTooltip

  (line 72):
   71        </SplitItem>
   72        <SplitItem>
   73          <HidableTooltip

  (line 89):
   88  
   89        <SplitItem>
   90          <Button onClick={onCancel} variant={ButtonVariant.link}>

  (line 94):
   93        </SplitItem>
   94        <SplitItem isFilled />
   95        <SplitItem>

  (line 95):
   94        <SplitItem isFilled />
   95        <SplitItem>
   96          <Button

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/views/catalog/CreateFromInstanceTypes/components/CreateVMFooter/components/ActionButtons/ActionButtons.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll analyze each fix carefully before making changes.

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant` type changed - but looking at the actual values used (`ButtonVariant.primary`, `ButtonVariant.secondary`, `ButtonVariant.link`), these are all still valid in the new type. The type just added `'stateful'`. No code changes needed for this fix.

**Fix 2**: Icon children to prop migration for `<Button>`. I need to check if any of these Button instances have icon children that need to be moved to the `icon` prop.

**Fix 3**: `SplitItem` new `component` prop. I need to review the usage pattern to see if this applies.

Let me read the file first.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Button.variant type change):** The type added `'stateful'` as a new valid value. The existing usages are `ButtonVariant.primary`, `ButtonVariant.secondary`, and `ButtonVariant.link` — all still valid. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2 (icon children to prop):** Looking at each Button:
- Line 62-69: `<Button ...>{t('Create VirtualMachine')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 78-85: `<Button ...>{t('Customize VirtualMachine')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 90-92: `<Button ...>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 96-102: `<Button ...>{t('View YAML & CLI')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**

None of the Button instances have icon children, so **no code changes needed for Fix 2**.

**Fix 3 (SplitItem `component` prop):** The new `component` prop on `SplitItem` is of type `React.ReactNode`. Looking at the SplitItem usages:
- Line 56: `<SplitItem>` wrapping `<HidableTooltip>` with a Button inside — standard children usage.
- Line 72: Same pattern.
- Line 89: `<SplitItem>` wrapping a single `<Button>`.
- Line 94: `<SplitItem isFilled />` — self-closing, no children.
- Line 95: `<SplitItem>` wrapping a single `<Button>`.

The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 is not meant to replace children in these cases — it's for specifying the HTML element type (like `component="span"` instead of the default `div`). The children pattern here is the standard usage. **No code change needed.**

All three fixes either don't require changes or the existing code is already compatible. The file needs no modifications.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type change): **No change needed.** The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new valid variant value. All existing usages (`ButtonVariant.primary`, `ButtonVariant.secondary`, `ButtonVariant.link`) remain valid in the new type.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **No change needed.** None of the four `<Button>` instances in this file contain icon elements as children — they all have only text content (via `{t('...')}`), so the icon-to-prop migration does not apply.

3. **Fix 3** (SplitItem new `component` prop): **No change needed.** The `component` prop on `SplitItem` is for specifying the rendered HTML element type, not for absorbing existing children content. The current children usage pattern (wrapping `<HidableTooltip>` and `<Button>` components) is the correct standard pattern and does not need migration.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1 (Button.variant type change):** The type added `'stateful'` as a new valid value. The existing usages are `ButtonVariant.primary`, `ButtonVariant.secondary`, and `ButtonVariant.link` — all still valid. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2 (icon children to prop):** Looking at each Button:
- Line 62-69: `<Button ...>{t('Create VirtualMachine')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 78-85: `<Button ...>{t('Customize VirtualMachine')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 90-92: `<Button ...>{t('Cancel')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 96-102: `<Button ...>{t('View YAML & CLI')}</Button>` — children is text only, no icon. **No change needed.**

None of the Button instances have icon children, so **no code changes needed for Fix 2**.

**Fix 3 (SplitItem `component` prop):** The new `component` prop on `SplitItem` is of type `React.ReactNode`. Looking at the SplitItem usages:
- Line 56: `<SplitItem>` wrapping `<HidableTooltip>` with a Button inside — standard children usage.
- Line 72: Same pattern.
- Line 89: `<SplitItem>` wrapping a single `<Button>`.
- Line 94: `<SplitItem isFilled />` — self-closing, no children.
- Line 95: `<SplitItem>` wrapping a single `<Button>`.

The `component` prop on `SplitItem` in PF v6 is not meant to replace children in these cases — it's for specifying the HTML element type (like `component="span"` instead of the default `div`). The children pattern here is the standard usage. **No code change needed.**

All three fixes either don't require changes or the existing code is already compatible. The file needs no modifications.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type change): **No change needed.** The type change only added `'stateful'` as a new valid variant value. All existing usages (`ButtonVariant.primary`, `ButtonVariant.secondary`, `ButtonVariant.link`) remain valid in the new type.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **No change needed.** None of the four `<Button>` instances in this file contain icon elements as children — they all have only text content (via `{t('...')}`), so the icon-to-prop migration does not apply.

3. **Fix 3** (SplitItem new `component` prop): **No change needed.** The `component` prop on `SplitItem` is for specifying the rendered HTML element type, not for absorbing existing children content. The current children usage pattern (wrapping `<HidableTooltip>` and `<Button>` components) is the correct standard pattern and does not need migration.
